title: Fat Loss – Non-Vegetarian
plateMethod: Fill half your plate with non-starchy vegetables, one-quarter with lean protein (chicken breast, fish, turkey, or legumes), and one-quarter with whole grains. Use small amounts of healthy fats.
hydration: Aim for 2–2.5 L of water per day. Drink a glass before meals to help with fullness.
sampleMeals: Breakfast: Oatmeal with berries and nuts; or eggs with spinach and whole-grain toast.
Lunch: Grilled chicken or fish salad with quinoa; or lean protein with brown rice and vegetables.
Dinner: Baked fish or chicken with mixed vegetables and a small portion of brown rice.
Snacks: Greek yogurt, fruit, vegetable sticks with hummus, or a small handful of nuts.
disclaimer: General guidance only; consult a healthcare or nutrition professional for personalized advice.
